papers.title: Second- and third-line treatment options for refractory monosymptomatic enuresis in children: a scoping review.
papers.authors: Selvi I, Marco BB, Dönmez Mİ, Baydilli N, Madarriaga YQ, Gernhold C et al.
papers.abstract: Around one-third of patients with monosymptomatic nocturnal enuresis (MNE) do not respond to conventional first-line treatments. Although international guidelines outline second- and third-line treatment options, these recommendations may not fully address the needs of every case encountered in daily practice. Therefore, the question of which treatment strategy should be used for refractory cases remains unanswered. We conducted a scoping review (PROSPERO CRD420251171197) following PRISMA Extension for Scoping Reviews (PRISMA-ScR), searching the PubMed, Embase, Ovid MEDLINE, Scopus, Web of Science, Google Scholar, CINAHL and the Cochrane Library databases to identify all published reports of treatment for refractory MNE in paediatric patients up to March 2026. Eligible studies included randomized controlled trials and prospective or retrospective observational cohort studies written in English. The primary outcome was the number of wet nights (classified as complete, partial or no response according to the ICCS criteria). Secondary outcomes included a comparison of the efficacy of second- and third-line treatment options if enough data could be found. The Cochrane RoB 2 tool was used to assess the risk of bias in randomized clinical trials, and the Methodological Index for Non-Randomized Studies (MINORS) was used for non-randomized cohort studies. Following screening and eligibility assessment, 17 out of 2578 articles met the PICO inclusion criteria. The included studies enrolled a total of 1348 children and adolescents with mean ages ranging from approximately 8 to 17 years. Many of the second- or third-line treatments described in the literature were excluded due to incorrect methodology, study design or patient population. The results showed that, following first-line treatment, a combination of biofeedback, electrical nerve stimulations, anticholinergics (e.g. oxybutynin, tolterodine, solifenacin), β3-adrenoceptor agonists (e.g. vibegron), tricyclic antidepressants (e.g. imipramine) and selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (e.g. fluoxetine) improved both partial response (PR) and complete response (CR) at varying rates across highly heterogeneous interventions and study populations. However, the evidence does not strongly support a treatment algorithm. Furthermore, reports on electro-acupuncture, furosemide, onabotulinumtoxin A injections or the herbal medicine 'shokenchuto' were considered unreliable for pure refractory MNE, despite improvements in PR and CR occurring at different rates. Conclusion: The question of which treatment strategy should be used for refractory cases remains unanswered due to a lack of sufficiently unbiased, prospective, randomized, controlled studies involving long-term follow-up. Some second- and third-line treatment options may improve PR and CR rates, despite the limited available evidence to support a treatment algorithm. However, further research is needed to confirm these benefits.
papers.title_ko: 소아 난치성 단순 야뇨증의 2차·3차 치료 विकल्प에 대한 범위 문헌고찰

summary.question: 첫 번째 치료에 반응하지 않는 소아 단순 야뇨증에서 어떤 2차·3차 치료 전략이 적절한지 평가하고자 했다.
summary.methods: 2026년 3월까지의 문헌을 PRISMA-ScR에 따라 검색한 범위 문헌고찰로, 무작위대조시험과 관찰연구를 포함해 치료 반응을 정리했다.
summary.results: 이질적인 일부 연구에서 바이오피드백, 전기신경자극, 항콜린제, β3 작용제, 삼환계 항우울제, SSRI 등이 부분반응과 완전반응을 개선했지만, 일관된 치료 알고리즘을 뒷받침할 근거는 부족했다.
summary.clinical_takeaway: 난치성 소아 단순 야뇨증에서 일부 2차·3차 치료가 반응률을 높일 수 있으나, 근거의 질과 일관성이 낮아 신중한 해석이 필요하다.
summary.limitations: 연구 간 이질성이 크고 장기 추적이 포함된 비뚤림이 적은 무작위 연구가 부족해 결론의 확실성이 낮다.

pico.p: 1차 치료에 반응하지 않는 소아·청소년 단순 야뇨증 환자
pico.i: 바이오피드백, 전기신경자극, 항콜린제, β3 작용제, 삼환계 항우울제, SSRI, 전침, 한약 등
pico.o: 야뇨 밤수 감소와 부분반응/완전반응

papers.title: [Micronutrients and phytotherapy in the response to stress].
papers.authors: Paré Vidal A, Alonso Osorio MªJ, Osorio Fernández G
papers.abstract: Introduction: micronutrients fulfil essential functions as cofactors and regulators of the metabolic pathways involved in the response to stress. When present in appropriate quantities, they help to maintain neuroendocrine and immune homeostasis in situations of high physiological demand. Adaptogens can normalize biological functions and strengthen systems affected by stress, protecting the organism against psychological and environmental insults. Objectives: to evaluate the bidirectional relationship between micronutrients and stress, with emphasis on the role of adaptogens. Methods: we reviewed the literature and selected relevant studies, mainly randomized controlled trials and systematic reviews and meta-analyses. Results: there is a bidirectional association between stress and micronutrient status: stress affects nutritional status via multiple mechanisms, including increased demand, reduced intake and absorption, and changes in eating habits. This could lead to micronutrient deficiency, which, in turn, compromises the body's response to stress. Conclusions: stress can exhaust our supply of specific micronutrients, and even slight deficiencies can affect energy metabolism, leading to tiredness, mental and physical fatigue, and a reduced ability to adapt to stress. Adaptogens can strengthen resistance to stress and prevent stress from becoming chronic. Supplementation with B vitamins and magnesium, together with vegetable extracts such as rhodiola and melissa, has proven beneficial in reducing stress and improving mood and well-being, with additional effects on energy and fatigue.
papers.title_ko: 미량영양소와 한약요법의 스트레스 반응에서의 역할

summary.question: 스트레스와 미량영양소 상태의 상호관계 및 적응제의 역할은 무엇인가?
summary.methods: 관련 문헌을 검토한 메타분석으로, 주로 무작위대조시험과 체계적 문헌고찰 및 메타분석을 선택해 종합했다.
summary.results: 스트레스는 미량영양소 상태를 악화시키고 결핍은 스트레스 적응을 저해하며, 비타민 B군·마그네슘과 로디올라·멜리사 추출물은 스트레스, 기분, 안녕감 개선에 유익한 것으로 보고됐다.
summary.clinical_takeaway: 스트레스 평가 시 미량영양소 결핍 가능성을 함께 고려하고, 적응성 약초와 영양중재의 근거는 문헌 수준에서 신중히 해석할 필요가 있다.
summary.limitations: 선정된 연구가 주로 이질적인 RCT와 체계적 문헌고찰이어서 직접 비교와 인과 해석에 주의가 필요하다.

pico.p: 스트레스 상황의 대상자
pico.o: 스트레스, 기분, 안녕감, 에너지, 피로
